demo.intro: Run the institute on live data — from the admin command center through sales and learner analytics, down to an AI-written report card on a student's phone.

steps.title: One command center, live all day
steps.text: The home dashboard adapts to each role — admins see institute-wide KPIs and finance, teachers see today's classes. Payments, doubts, live classes and real-time usage all land on one screen.

steps.title: KPIs with deltas, not spreadsheets
steps.text: Enrollments, fee collection, learners online this minute and unresolved doubts — every headline number carries its change over the period, so trends are obvious without opening a report.

steps.title: See when your learners actually study
steps.text: Real-time usage widgets chart hourly and daily activity, most active learners and the web/mobile/desktop device split — so you schedule live classes when students are genuinely online.

steps.title: A dozen sales reports, one filter bar
steps.text: Overview, sources, funnel, revenue, cohorts and forecast tabs share the same date, team and counsellor filters. The active tab lives in the URL, so any view is a shareable deep link.

steps.title: Know exactly where leads drop off
steps.text: The funnel report tracks every stage from first enquiry to admission. Here, 412 demo bookings became 148 admissions — the drop after application is where counsellors should focus.

steps.title: Revenue attributed to every source
steps.text: Meta, Google, website and walk-in leads are compared on volume, admissions and actual ₹ revenue. Every tab exports to CSV, and PDF reports go out under your own letterhead.

steps.title: Spot engagement dips before dropouts
steps.text: Daily activity trends per batch reveal momentum or drop-off early — like this mid-month slump. Drill from batch to subject, chapter and even slide-level progress per learner.

steps.title: At-risk learners flag themselves
steps.text: Login recency, study-time trends and leaderboard ranks combine into per-learner flags. Daily, weekly and monthly email digests then keep parents informed automatically — no one lifts a finger.

steps.title: An AI report card, one click away
steps.text: For any student, AI pulls attendance, marks, course progress, assignments, live-class participation, doubts and login habits into one report — readable in the app or emailed to parents as a branded PDF.

steps.title: Insights a teacher would write
steps.text: The AI adds a plain-language narrative with per-topic confidence ratings — what's strong, what needs work, and the habits behind the numbers. Learners open every report from their own 'My Reports' hub.
